notes: GoHighLevel integration — phase 1: website form submissions become GHL leads automatically. Turn it on in Plugins → GoHighLevel (Super-Admin only): paste a Private Integration Token from your GHL sub-account (Settings → Private Integrations, with the Contacts scope) plus your Location ID, and flip it on. From then on, every submission on that site is pushed into GoHighLevel as a contact — first/last name, email, and phone are detected automatically, the lead is tagged 'Website Lead' + the form name, and the full submission is attached as a note. No need to rebuild your forms in GHL. It de-duplicates by email/phone (a repeat submitter updates rather than duplicates), and it's best-effort — a GHL hiccup never blocks the form or its email notification. Security: the token is stored encrypted on the server and never reaches the browser or the site's repo; only Super-Admins can see or change the connection. There's a 'Test connection' button, and an on/off switch so you can enable it per client (it's a paid add-on). Coming next (phase 2): a dashboard that pulls your GHL leads and chat interactions back into the CMS, with CSV export. Previous (1.14.41) — reCAPTCHA v3 support (invisible, score-based, with the badge) and an adjustable block threshold. In Plugins → reCAPTCHA you can now choose the version: v2 — the “I’m not a robot” checkbox (still the default, so existing sites are untouched) — or v3, which runs invisibly, shows Google’s small “protected by reCAPTCHA” badge in the corner, and scores every submission in the background. With v3 you get a Block Threshold slider (0–1, default 0.5): submissions scoring below it are treated as bots and blocked — raise it toward 0.8–0.9 for stricter filtering, lower it to be more lenient. The score is enforced on the server, so it can’t be bypassed. Two things to know when switching to v3: it needs its OWN keys (choose reCAPTCHA v3 when you register the site at google.com/recaptcha/admin — v2 keys won’t work for v3), and the visible checkbox is replaced by the corner badge. Previous (1.14.40) — Revisions: View and Restore actually work now. If you clicked View or Restore on a saved version and nothing happened, this is the fix. Both used to depend on a browser pop-up (View) and the browser's built-in confirmation dialog (Restore) — and some browsers, especially in-app/embedded ones and strict pop-up blockers, silently block both, so the buttons looked dead even though the code was running. Now View opens the saved version in a preview window right inside the CMS (with an 'Open in new tab' option), and Restore asks you to confirm with an in-CMS dialog — neither relies on browser pop-ups or dialogs anymore. And if a saved snapshot is ever missing, you get a clear message in the preview instead of silence. Previous (1.14.39) — Top-bar cleanup, forms CSS is now what-you-see-is-what-you-get, page versions are backed up in GitHub, and reCAPTCHA is a proper plugin. (1) Top bar — the search box (which didn't do anything) is gone, and the site name at the top is now a simple link: click it to open your live site in a new tab, or — while you're editing a page — that exact page (same target as the Preview button). The little down-arrow is gone. (2) Forms — the Custom CSS box on a form is now the single source of truth for that form's styling. Before, each Save added your CSS as a new block stacked on top of the old one, so removing a line didn't actually remove it (a red border you added and then deleted would 'stick'). Now Save replaces the form's style block with exactly what's in the box — add, edit, or delete freely and the live page matches. (Also hardened so this can never touch the rest of the page's markup.) (3) Revisions fixed, and page versions are now backed up in GitHub. First the fix: the View and Restore buttons never actually did anything — the button's click handler was malformed by a quoting bug, so clicking it silently errored out — and editors couldn't open the Revisions panel at all (it was gated to admins). Both are fixed: View now opens the saved version in a new tab and Restore redeploys it to the live page, for admins and editors alike. Second, the backup: every time you Save a page the CMS already stored a full snapshot behind those buttons; those snapshots (and the revision index) are now also mirrored into your site's GitHub repository — so your page version history lives in the repo, your backup of record, and survives even if the server is rebuilt. Older snapshots are pruned from GitHub too, so the repo stays tidy. (This uses the same GitHub repo + token connection that already publishes your pages — set it in Settings; the mirror is best-effort and never blocks a save.) (4) reCAPTCHA is now a proper add-on in the Plugins section (like a Gravity Forms add-on) instead of being buried in Site Foundry: turn it on, paste your two Google keys once, and every form on the site is protected — with an at-a-glance Active/Off badge, a one-click 'Turn off', and a link to grab your keys. Previous (1.14.38) — Editors get Forms + Revisions, and the Revisions history works for everyone. (1) Editor accounts now include Forms (build forms, view submissions) and Revisions (view history, restore versions) by default — no admin toggle needed. An admin can still switch either off for a specific editor. (2) Fixed — the Revisions list was admin-only even when the Revisions module was granted; now anyone with that module sees it. (3) Fixed — the 'View' button on a revision opened a blank tab (the preview window was opened a moment too late, so the browser's pop-up blocker killed it). It now opens reliably; Restore is unchanged. Previous (1.14.37) — Already-launched sites keep sending form email with zero setup. If a site still has its old contact-form handler (a send.php with SMTP credentials) from before it was moved onto the CMS, the CMS now reads those mail credentials straight out of that file — so its contact form keeps emailing without you touching a single config file on that site. (The old send.php is read as text and never run; anything you set in config.secret.php still takes priority.) Previous (1.14.36) — Contact-form email is more forgiving to set up, plus a cleaner config template. When a site sends form email over SMTP, the 'From' address now uses your configured mg_from; if that's left blank it falls back to your SMTP login (always a valid sender) instead of the built-in example address — which the mail service would reject. So a form no longer silently fails just because the From line wasn't filled in. The private-config template (config.secret.example.php) was also rewritten to be much easier to follow, with copy-across instructions for moving a site off an old PHPMailer 'send.php' (which fields map where). Previous (1.14.35) — One-step publishing — 'Save' now applies to every page, no second click. Setting up Analytics used to take two steps: save your tracking IDs (or custom code), then hunt for a separate 'Apply Tracking to All Pages' button. Now clicking Save publishes it to every page in one action — the extra button is gone, and the buttons read 'Save & Apply to All Pages' so it's clear what they do. The Favicon works the same way: uploading (or removing) it now updates every page automatically instead of making you click a second 'Apply to all pages' button. ('Remove from All Pages' for tracking is still there for when you want to strip it.) Previous (1.14.34) — Form builder: a header, visual style controls, and content blocks (Gravity-Forms-style). (1) Header — a form now has its own Heading and Intro text (in the builder's 'Form Header' card) that show above the fields. When you convert a form that's already on a page, the CMS now keeps the heading/intro that sat with it instead of dropping them. (2) Styles — a new 'Styles' card lets you set the form's background color, field corner radius, button color, label color, and input border color, and toggle 'Show field labels' on or off (when off, each field's label becomes its placeholder) — all without writing any CSS. (The Custom CSS box is still there for advanced tweaks, applied on top.) (3) Content blocks — the Add Field palette has a new 'Layout & Content' group with Heading and Text blocks (plus the existing Section Break and HTML Block), so you can add titles and explanatory text between fields. Everything shows up on the live page when you Save, and re-saving pushes the new look to every page the form is on. Previous (1.14.33) — api.php now updates itself, so server-side fixes reach every site automatically. Until now, admin/api.php was the one engine file that never auto-updated — so fixes that live in it (like the contact-form fix, SMTP sending, and clean URLs) had to be uploaded to each site by hand. Because api.php no longer contains any secrets (they're all read from the private config.secret.php file), it can now ride the same automatic updater as the rest of the CMS. IMPORTANT — one last manual step: deploy THIS release's admin/api.php to each site once by hand; from then on api.php updates on its own along with everything else. Safety: the updater will only overwrite api.php on sites that use config.secret.php — if a site still has its secrets written directly inside api.php, the update is refused for that file so nothing is ever wiped (move those secrets into config.secret.php to turn auto-update on for it). Previous (1.14.32) — Fix — side-by-side (half-width) form fields now actually show up on your live pages. Two things were stopping them: (1) pages where a form was placed before the half-width feature kept an older copy of the form's built-in styling, and the CMS wasn't refreshing it — so the new side-by-side layout had no CSS to work with. Now, every time you Save a form, the CMS refreshes that styling on each page the form appears on. (2) Some site themes were overriding the form layout and flattening the fields back into one column; the side-by-side rules are now enforced (scoped and marked important) so a theme can't override them. If your fields are still stacked after this update, open the form and click Save once to push the fix onto your pages. Previous (1.14.31) — Per-page CSS editor now shows the page's existing styling. Open any page's settings (the gear on a page), and the Custom CSS box is pre-filled with the CSS that's already on that page — pulled from the page's own <style> blocks — so you can see and edit your page's styling in one place instead of starting from a blank box. Anything you type is layered on top as an override (so your changes win over the original) and is written into the page file when you Save the page. The box only shows your page's real CSS: the CMS's own managed styles and the shared contact-form styling are left out. (This complements the per-form Custom CSS box from 1.14.30 — that one styles a specific form; this one styles the whole page.) Previous (1.14.30) — Form editor now updates the live form, plus a form CSS editor. (1) When you edit a form and click Save, the CMS re-renders that form everywhere it's already placed on your site — so field changes and styling actually show up on the page (previously an edit only saved inside the CMS and the page kept the old form). (2) The form builder has a new Custom CSS box: style the form yourself (target .foundry-form) to change the button, input borders, spacing, colors, etc. — it's saved with the form and applied wherever the form appears. Previous (1.14.29) — Form builder: drag-and-drop, side-by-side fields, and rounded inputs. Grab the handle (⠿) on any field to drag it up or down and reorder your form. Each field now has a Full width / Half width choice — two half-width fields sit next to each other, so you can build layouts like First Name | Last Name and Phone | Email with a full-width Message and Submit below. Form inputs also have nicely rounded corners now. (Building or re-saving a form applies the new layout; on phones, side-by-side fields stack automatically.) Previous (1.14.28) — Critical fix — contact form submissions are now received. The server was rejecting every public form submission (visitors don't have an admin login), so nothing reached the CMS: no entries, no email notifications. This fixes the form endpoint so submissions come through, get saved, and appear in Forms → Entries. IMPORTANT: this is a server-side fix in api.php — deploy the updated admin/api.php to each site for forms to start working (api.php never auto-updates). Previous (1.14.27) — Form entries fixes + convert existing forms. (1) Submissions now always show up in Entries, even ones that came in without a form ID — they're grouped under 'Uncategorized' so nothing is hidden. (2) New: turn a form that's already on one of your pages into a managed form. In the Forms tab click 'Convert a page form', pick the form the CMS found, review the fields it detected, and Save — the CMS rebuilds it as a proper form and swaps it onto the page, so from then on its submissions are captured and appear in Entries (and export to CSV). This is the piece that was missing when a site was moved onto the CMS: the original contact form kept posting to its old handler, so nothing reached the CMS. IMPORTANT: for captured submissions to be emailed to you, set up SMTP or Mailgun in the site's config (see 1.14.26). Previous (1.14.26) — Contact forms can now send email through SMTP. If a site uses an SMTP mail service (like Mailgun's SMTP), the CMS can send form submissions straight through it — just add the SMTP host, username, and password to the site's private config file. This makes moving an existing site's contact form onto the CMS much easier: the credentials it already had keep working, no separate API key needed. (Setup is server-side; nothing changes in how you use the CMS day to day.) Previous (1.14.25) — Media Library: a much nicer alt-text editor. Clicking an image now opens a full-size popup showing the whole photo, with a large, easy-to-use alt-text box and left/right arrows to move through your library. Saving (or clicking 'Save & next') writes the alt text onto your pages automatically — the separate 'Push alt to pages' button is gone. Only images you actually change are re-applied, so paging through is quick. Previous (1.14.24) — Alt text in the Media Library. Click any image in the Media Library to add alt text — a short description of the image used by screen readers and search engines (good for accessibility and SEO). It's saved with the image, and the new 'Push alt to pages' button writes it onto every matching image across all your pages at once — it finds the image wherever it's used and updates its alt text, while leaving unrelated images alone. Your alt text is kept even when you re-scan the media library. Previous (1.14.23) — Form entries + CSV export. The Forms tab now shows how many submissions each form has received; click that number to open all of a form's entries in a table (newest first), with a 'Download CSV' button to export them to a spreadsheet (Excel/Google Sheets). Submissions that came in from a form not built in the CMS — like an existing hand-coded contact form on your site — are surfaced too, so no entries get missed. (Entries were already being captured on the server; this adds the viewing + downloading.) Previous (1.14.22) — Fix — the accessibility widget no longer overlaps the reCAPTCHA badge. On pages with reCAPTCHA (which puts a small badge in a bottom corner), the ADAptify accessibility button now automatically moves up to sit just above the badge instead of on top of it. It only shifts when it shares a corner with the badge — otherwise it stays exactly where you placed it — and it adjusts even if the badge loads a moment after the page. Previous (1.14.21) — Team onboarding for agency-managed sites (optional, server-configured). Lets your team self-provision editor logins across client sites: the first time someone signs in with their agency email and the shared onboarding password, an editor account is created for them and they're prompted to set their own password. It never overrides an existing account and only ever grants editor-level access. There's no change to how you use the CMS day to day. Previous (1.14.20) — Fix — clean (no-.html) page links now work automatically. When your site uses short, extensionless web addresses (like /contact instead of /contact.html), the CMS now installs the matching rule on your web server for you, so those links open the right page instead of a 'Not Found' error. It runs automatically when you open the CMS and whenever you use SEO → 'Apply to all pages'. IMPORTANT: this needs the updated admin/api.php deployed to your site once (api.php never updates itself) — until it's deployed, add the clean-URL rewrite block to your site's root .htaccess by hand. This is what caused extensionless links to 404 on a live site: the CMS had rewritten every link to the short form, but the server had never been told to serve /page from /page.html. Previous (1.14.19) — Maintenance — automatic updates now come from the agency's renamed shared account. The account that publishes CMS engine updates was renamed to YourDigitalGroup, so every deployed site's auto-updater has been re-pointed to it. Nothing changes in how you use the CMS; this just makes sure future updates keep arriving. (The 'Repository Override' and 'Create New Repo' placeholders were updated to the new account name too.) Previous (1.14.18) — Style individual words (inline text formatting). Double-click any text on your page to edit it, then select a word or phrase and a small toolbar appears right above it — set bold, italic, or underline, change the font or size, or pick a color for just that selection (like Google Docs). Press Escape or click elsewhere to finish. Chosen fonts load automatically so they look right in the editor and on the live page. Previous (1.14.17) — Icon library + SVG uploads. In the page editor, select an element and click 'Insert an icon' to open a searchable library of common icons (contact, arrows, checkmarks, shopping, social, and more) — pick one and it drops into your page, ready to recolor and resize like any other element. The page automatically loads the icon font so your icons show up both in the editor and on the live site. You can also upload your own SVG graphics through the Media Library (and the editor's image picker) and use them like any other image. Previous (1.14.16) — Dark mode fix — the editor's Markup button is readable again. In dark mode it was showing white text on a light button; its text now stays dark on the light button (and white on the orange hover), so it reads clearly in both light and dark. Previous (1.14.15) — Site-wide heading styles, friendlier wording, and editor polish. (1) The Design tab now has Heading Styles — choose the font, size, weight, alignment, and color for your titles (H1 through H6) across your whole site, then click Apply to all pages. A single page can still set its own heading look in that page's settings. (2) The help text throughout the CMS has been rewritten in plain, everyday language — less technical jargon, more about what each setting actually does for you. (3) In the page editor's Background control, 'No fill' is now a swatch (a box with a red diagonal line) that sits right in the color row, like a design app, instead of a separate button. (4) Removed the dashboard's 'Team activity' and 'Top performer' placeholder panels so your pages list uses the full width. Previous (1.14.14) — Clean URLs are now always on. The brief on/off toggle from 1.14.13 is gone — the CMS simply always uses extensionless URLs, so there's nothing to switch. After deploying your server's URL rewrite, run 'Apply to all pages' in SEO to rewrite existing pages. Previous (1.14.13) — Clean URLs — drop the .html for better SEO. Page URLs are now extensionless (e.g. /about instead of /about.html): each page gets one canonical, search-friendly address, the sitemap and the page's own internal links use that clean form, and any old .html link redirects to it automatically. Run 'Apply to all pages' in SEO to rewrite your existing pages' links and canonicals to the clean form. This needs the matching URL rewrite on your server, included with this release's site configuration. Your files stay plain .html on disk — only the public URL changes. Previous (1.14.12) — Editor controls: collapsible panels, border, background options, and a sticky-header toggle. (1) The element properties panel is now organized into collapsible sections — Text Style, Spacing & Style, Border, Size, Anchor, Hover, and more — so you can fold away what you're not using. Each section remembers whether you left it open or closed. (2) New Border controls: width, style (solid / dashed / dotted / double), and color; setting a width auto-applies a solid style so the border shows right away. Like the Size controls, borders honor the per-device cascade (set them differently for desktop, tablet, or mobile). (3) The Background control gains two options: a 'No fill' button to make a section transparent, and an 'Image' button to set a background image from the Media Library (with a 'Remove image' once one is set). (4) Header elements get a 'Sticky' toggle — turn it on so the header stays visible as visitors scroll, then use Save to All to apply it across every page. Previous (1.14.11) — Dark mode for the CMS. The admin now has a dark theme — open the profile menu (the avatar in the top-right) and flip the Dark mode switch. Your choice is remembered on this device and applied before the screen draws, so there's no flash of the light theme when you reload; if you've never picked one, it follows your operating system's light/dark setting. The whole interface re-themes — sidebar, top bar, panels, cards, inputs, modals, dropdowns, and the editor canvas — with the ember accent kept throughout. The page preview itself stays on light 'paper' so the website you're editing always looks the way visitors will see it. Previous (1.14.10) — Responsive editing fixes + size controls + jump-to-code. (1) Style changes now apply correctly across devices. Setting a size, spacing, or other style on Desktop or Tablet no longer locks that value so it can't be overridden on smaller screens. Every device value is now written into one managed stylesheet in cascade order Desktop -> Tablet -> Mobile (same specificity), so a value set on Mobile wins on phones; a value set on Tablet applies to tablet and phones unless Mobile overrides it; and a Desktop value applies everywhere unless Tablet or Mobile changes it. Any older inline value that was trapping a property at one breakpoint is migrated into the Desktop rule the first time you edit that property, so margins, padding, width and height finally respond on mobile. (2) New Size controls in the element panel: Width, Max width, Height and Max height inputs for any element - divs, images, videos, columns - accepting px, %, auto, and the like. They honor the same per-device cascade, so you can size something one way on desktop and another on mobile, and 'Reset to natural size' now clears all four across every device. (3) Switching a selected element to Code view now jumps to its markup: open the Code editor with an element selected and it scrolls straight to where that element is in the source and briefly highlights it, so you don't have to hunt for it. Previous (1.14.9) — Pages can no longer silently vanish from the editor list. The list is built from pages.json plus a server scan of your files; if that scan fails or times out (for example on a site whose data/ folder has filled up with revision snapshots) while pages.json is sparse, the list could rebuild empty and look like every page was dropped. The CMS now keeps a 'last known good' snapshot of your page list and, whenever a read it depends on fails, restores any page that was dropped (still respecting pages you've hidden, and still clearing on a genuine empty result). The snapshot is captured on every import, page save, and healthy load, so it survives refreshes — and intentional deletions are kept in sync so they don't come back. (The underlying cause on affected sites is the data/ revision build-up; the v1.14.7 api.php change stops the scanner reading data/ — deploy it to clear the root cause.) Previous (1.14.8) — Imports no longer use the Markup engine — no API tokens are spent when importing. Bringing pages into the CMS (scanning from the server or dropping a ZIP) is now a plain, fast operation: each page is read and stored as-is, with its CSS still captured so styling is preserved, and no per-page AI calls. The 'Optimize markup for editing' option and the automatic AI section-splitting have both been removed from the import flow (do that refactoring in Claude Design instead). The in-editor Markup tools are unchanged. Previous (1.14.7) — Imported pages no longer drop off — root-cause fix. The CMS writes a revision snapshot on every page save, stored in your site's data/ folder (data/rev_rev_<timestamp>.html). The page scanner was reading that folder, so those revision files showed up as 'pages' and then vanished on the next load — the editor correctly refuses to keep data files — which looked exactly like your imported pages being dropped. Now: the page scanner no longer looks inside data/ at all (it never did belong there), the page list already ignores anything under data/, and — as a safety net — if the page list ever fails to load because of a momentary network or session hiccup, the CMS keeps the pages already on screen instead of blanking the list. NOTE: the scanner change is in api.php, which is a manual deploy — deploy the updated admin/api.php to get the full root-cause fix; until then the page list still hides those files client-side, so you won't see them, and a hard-refresh ensures you're on this build. Previous (1.14.6) — Import fixes + click-anywhere rows. (1) Site Foundry no longer offers — or keeps — files that aren't real, editable pages. Data files, CMS revision snapshots (e.g. data/rev_rev_1782506528689.html), and backup files are filtered out of the scan and skipped on import (the scan now shows how many system/backup files were skipped). This is what was causing imported pages to 'disappear' after you left the editor: those entries were revision files the editor can't open, so they dropped off on the next scan — the pages list is now stable, and any such junk already saved in your pages list is cleaned up automatically on load. (2) Click anywhere on a row to act on it: the Site Foundry import scan rows now toggle their checkbox on a row click (not just the box), and the Users, Posts, and Forms lists open the editor on a row click (not just the Edit button) — controls inside a row (Edit, copy-shortcode, entry links) still do their own thing. Previous (1.14.5) — Import progress loader. Site Foundry's 'Import from Server' now shows a clear, animated progress indicator while it runs — a spinner, 'Importing page X of N' with the live step (reading / optimizing / extracting sections / saving), and a progress bar that fills as pages complete — plus a spinner on the Import button. So it's obvious the import is working and not stuck, and the loader clears the moment it finishes. Previous (1.14.4) — Import reliability fix. Site Foundry imports no longer get stuck on the 'Importing…' spinner. The per-page Markup calls (block extraction, and the optional page optimization) now have a client-side timeout, so a slow or unavailable Markup engine can never freeze an import — that page falls back to importing as-is and the run continues. The 'Optimize markup for editing' option is now OFF by default (opt-in) on both Site Foundry tabs: running the full optimization on every page made imports slow and look hung, so it's now a deliberate choice — turn it on when you want it (it processes each page through Markup, so it takes longer). Previous (1.14.3) — Editor image previews + cleaner color swatches. The 'Site Colors' captions under the editor's color pickers are gone — just the clickable swatches remain, sitting directly under each color field. And image previews in the page editor are now clear, square cards: when an image is selected, its preview at the top of the properties panel is a square box (was a short strip that squished wide logos/banners to ~50px tall), and the image picker's tiles use a square thumbnail with object-fit: contain so the full image is always visible regardless of its shape — much easier to see and pick. Previous (1.14.2) — Site color palette in the editor. Wherever you set a color in the page editor — text color, background, and button/link hover colors — a 'site colors' palette now appears beneath the picker: the colors actually used on the page, listed most-used to least-used, with your brand colors always included. Click a swatch to apply it instantly, so it's easy to stay on-palette without copying hex codes. (The palette reads the live page's rendered colors and refreshes when you open a different page.) Previous (1.14.1) — Editor freshness + SEO accuracy + Design polish. (1) The page editor now always opens the freshest published version of a page — it cache-busts the file when loading, so a stale browser-cached copy can no longer show up after you publish and then refresh the CMS. (2) The SEO analysis is fairer and more technical, closer to a Lighthouse audit. Keyword matching is no longer punctuation-literal: a focus keyword like 'doors vermillion sd' now correctly matches a title of 'Doors, Vermillion, SD' (and partial word matches count, so it isn't overly strict about exact phrasing). The checklist adds technical health checks read from the live page — descriptive (non-placeholder) title, indexable (not noindex), canonical available, exactly one H1, all images have alt text, and enough content — alongside the keyword checks. Generated SEO now produces a clean primary keyword (lowercase, no punctuation) whose words appear naturally in the title and description. (3) The Design tab's Primary and Secondary brand-color pickers are now proper squares instead of wide, short boxes. (4) Client-facing wording no longer mentions 'Claude' or an API key: Site Foundry shows a plain 'Markup isn't enabled yet' note instead of 'No Claude API key — add it in Settings', and the editor's status and error messages use 'Markup'. Previous (1.14.0) — Automatic markup optimization in Site Foundry. When you bring a site into the CMS — either by scanning the files on your server or by dropping a ZIP — each page's markup can now be optimized for the editor: semantic landmarks (header / nav / main / footer), named sections with clear ids, the standard FAQ/accordion structure, proper headings (one H1 per page), real image tags with alt text, a single place for CSS, brand-color variables, and clean file naming (index.html for the homepage and a single .html extension for the rest). A new 'Optimize markup for editing' toggle (on by default, shown when Markup is available) controls it on both Site Foundry tabs. Your design is never changed: every optimized page is run through a fidelity check first — if its visible text or its stylesheet didn't survive intact, the original page is kept untouched — and the page's scripts are preserved verbatim across the round trip. When scanning from the server, the optimized page is written back to the server file (and pushed to GitHub when connected) so the editor opens the improved version; pages too large to optimize safely, or that don't pass the check, are imported as-is. Previous (1.13.8) — Faithful mobile/tablet preview + reachable controls. The Tablet and Mobile previews now emulate a real device like the browser's inspector: the page renders at the device's true CSS width (mobile 390×844, tablet 768×1024) so its own responsive CSS reflows exactly as on the device, and the whole frame scales down to fit the editor — much closer to what you see on your phone. Editing handles now stay grabbable even when an element runs off the edge of a narrow preview: the resize handles clamp to the visible edge instead of disappearing off-screen (and the move/duplicate/delete + size controls in the right panel are always available). Previous (1.13.7) — Tidy-up: the Hover controls in the editor are now labeled just 'Background' and 'Text' (they already sit under the 'Hover' heading, so the repeated word is gone). Previous (1.13.6) — Editor breathing room + privacy-page layout. Generated pages (privacy policy, new pages) now place the content in a centered column with real side margins whose layout is locked with !important so the site's own CSS can't stretch it edge-to-edge, and the real header and footer are kept inside their original wrapper (so their styling holds) — the content between them is swapped rather than rebuilt. The main left sidebar now auto-collapses when you enter the page editor (more room for the preview) and expands again elsewhere; you can still toggle it any time with the button by the logo. The desktop preview is now resizable — grab the orange handle at its bottom-right corner and drag to set any size (switching device resets it). Previous (1.13.5) — Font picker upgrades for per-page heading styles. Each font in the dropdown now renders in its own typeface (the curated fonts load into the admin so you can see them). Added a 'More fonts…' option that opens a searchable picker for the entire Google Fonts library — type to filter, each result previews in its font, and you can apply any font by exact name even if it's not in the list (the browse list is fetched live, with the built-in set as a fallback). Picked fonts are added to the dropdown and load on the page automatically. Previous (1.13.4) — Editor polish + fixes. The page editor's properties panel no longer has an 'Apply Changes' button — every edit (text, color, size, weight, alignment, spacing, links, anchors, image source/alt) now applies automatically as you make it. Text alignment now applies reliably (written with !important so a theme's own rules can't override it). Buttons and links gain Hover controls — set a hover background and text color, saved as a per-element :hover rule in the page. Per-page heading styles now cover H1–H6 (was H1–H3) and add a Google Fonts picker; the chosen fonts are loaded on the page automatically. New pages and the privacy-policy page now sit in a centered content section with real side margins and room below the header (no more overlap), while still pulling in the site's real header and footer. Fixed the Restore button in Site Foundry → Manage Pages (it wasn't firing due to a quote-escaping bug) and removed the now-redundant remove buttons there. Reworded client-facing messages so they no longer reference internal system files. Previous (1.13.3) — Subtle version stamp in the bottom-left of the sidebar (small gray monospace, e.g. 'v1.13.3') so you can see which build is loaded at a glance — it reads the live CMS_VERSION and hides when the sidebar is collapsed. Previous (1.13.2) — New + generated pages now inherit the site header/footer. The privacy-policy generator and the New Page action used to emit a standalone document with no header/nav/footer; they now clone a real page (its head/CSS + header + nav + footer) and swap in the new content, so created pages match the rest of the site (falls back to a standalone doc only if there's no donor page). Resize anchoring fixed properly: a normal drag now keeps the OPPOSITE edge/corner fixed (drag the bottom-right and the left/top stay put) no matter how the element is centered — it probes how margin maps to on-screen position, then measures and corrects so the anchor holds even for flex- or text-align-centered elements (the old margin-pin didn't work on those, so it looked symmetric). Hold Cmd (Mac) / Ctrl (PC) for symmetric resize from the center. Site Foundry → Manage Pages: removed the redundant 'Remove from CMS' buttons (removal lives in the editor's gear → Page Settings); the page list + Restore for hidden pages remain. The sidebar 'Pages' count (and dashboard stat) now update immediately whenever a page is removed, deleted, restored, or added. Design tab: brand colors are now Primary + Secondary, bound to the --brand-color-primary / --brand-color-secondary CSS variables. They auto-fill from the site's CSS (and empty Contact/Social fields auto-fill from the site's links); 'Apply colors to all pages' injects a managed :root override into every page so the variables update site-wide. Previous (1.13.1) — Fixes. Remove-from-CMS (Site Foundry → Manage Pages, and the Page Settings gear): now matches the page by its derived id (pageIdFromPath normalizes the path deterministically) plus a normalized full-path backstop, instead of brittle filename/path string compares — so removal sticks for subdirectory pages and survives path-format drift (case, leading ./ or /). It no longer wrongly hides a different page that happens to share a filename (e.g. services/plumbing.html vs plumbing.html), and loadPages now makes sure site.json is loaded before filtering so a removed page can't reappear when the editor opens before the Design tab. Div/container resize handles: each drag now sets an absolute size from a fresh snapshot and the element no longer re-centers between drags — margin-left/top are pinned on the active axis so an auto-centered element (margin:0 auto) stays put. Default drag anchors the opposite edge/corner; hold Cmd (Mac) / Ctrl (PC) to resize symmetrically from the center (opposite edge moves equally), like Photoshop. Previous (1.13.0) — Password-protect pages (PHP session gate). Page Settings now has a 'Password-protect this page' toggle + password field. When enabled, api.php stores a bcrypt hash of the password in admin/protect.secret.php (a PHP file, never served as source), (re)writes a public _fourge_gate.php, and maintains a managed RewriteRule block in the root .htaccess so the page routes through the gate. Visitors get a branded password screen; on the correct password the gate sets a PHP session cookie and serves the page (path-traversal-safe, only pages in the protect list are servable, wrong attempts are slowed and the hash never reaches the browser). Protected pages show a lock icon in the editor's page list. NOTE: requires the updated api.php deployed by hand (api.php is always a manual deploy); the gate file + .htaccess rules are written automatically the first time you protect a page. Previous (1.12.0) — Responsive editing (Elementor-style funnel) + reverse columns. The Desktop/Tablet/Mobile toggle in the editor now scopes your edits: switch to Tablet or Mobile and any style change (font size, color, weight, alignment, spacing, radius, background, width/height — including drag-resize) is written as a per-device media-query rule instead of an inline style, keyed to a stable per-element class. Values funnel down the cascade — Desktop applies everywhere, Tablet overrides tablet+mobile (≤1024px), Mobile overrides mobile (≤600px) — so you only override what differs. The properties panel shows a banner telling you which device you're editing. Flex containers get a Direction control (row / row-reverse / column / column-reverse); set it per device to reverse or stack columns on phones. Overrides are saved into a managed <style id=fourge-responsive> block in the page and the element classes, so they reconstruct correctly when you reopen the page (and a new override never wipes existing ones). Previous (1.11.0) — Collapsible side menus + drag-to-resize containers. The Desktop/Tablet/Mobile toggle in the editor now scopes your edits: switch to Tablet or Mobile and any style change (font size, color, weight, alignment, spacing, radius, background, width/height — including drag-resize) is written as a per-device media-query rule instead of an inline style, keyed to a stable per-element class. Values funnel down the cascade — Desktop applies everywhere, Tablet overrides tablet+mobile (≤1024px), Mobile overrides mobile (≤600px) — so you only override what differs. The properties panel shows a banner telling you which device you're editing. Flex containers get a Direction control (row / row-reverse / column / column-reverse); set it per device to reverse or stack columns on phones. Overrides are saved into a managed <style id=fourge-responsive> block in the page and the element classes, so they reconstruct correctly when you reopen the page (and a new override never wipes existing ones). Previous (1.11.0) — Collapsible side menus + drag-to-resize containers. Both side menus now collapse: a toggle by the logo collapses the main sidebar, and a toggle in the editor toolbar collapses the page list in the page editor — each slides to zero width and the choice persists per browser. Drag-to-resize: selecting a container that holds text (or an image) in the visual editor now shows eight resize handles (four corners + four edge midpoints). Drag any handle to resize — corners resize both dimensions, edges resize one; dragging the left/top edges keeps the opposite edge anchored (margin compensation). The new width/height (and any margin) are written as inline styles on the element, so the change is saved accurately into the page's HTML. A resize is one undo step. Previous (1.10.0) — Editor + site polish from the testing meeting. Links: the properties panel now has an 'Open in a new tab' checkbox for any link (sets target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer) and clears it cleanly when unchecked. Anchors: every element (especially sections and columns) now has an editable 'Anchor ID' field, so you can link to a spot on the page with #id — the id is sanitized to a valid anchor as you type, and the panel shows the exact #link to use. Favicon: Design → Design now has a Favicon uploader (square PNG/SVG/ICO) with a live preview; 'Apply to all pages' injects a managed <link rel=icon> (+ apple-touch-icon) into every page and Remove clears it. Per-page heading styles: Page Settings now has H1/H2/H3 overrides (size, weight, alignment, color) that compile to scoped, !important CSS injected only into that page on Save, so a page can override the site design without touching global styles. Go Live tool: Settings now has a 'Find & Replace URL' card (Architect only) to swap a staging/preview domain for the production domain across all pages, posts, sitemap.xml and robots.txt — with a non-destructive Preview (per-file occurrence counts) before applying, and it also fixes the website URL in site.json. Renamed the last visible 'SITE FACTORY' label to 'SITE FOUNDRY'. Previous (1.9.0) — Page management: each page in the editor now has a gear → Page Settings (Custom Page CSS + Custom Page JavaScript injected into that page only on save, Draft toggle that noindexes + drops the page from the sitemap, Delete page, and Remove-from-CMS). Targeted-landing pages now show a red dot in the page list. Fixed Remove-from-CMS not sticking (the hidden-page match now checks path OR file OR id). Polish: the SEO Generate buttons now read 'Generate' / 'Generate All' (dropped 'with AI') with a clean Font Awesome icon instead of the sparkle emoji — no emojis in the UI. Renamed the Site Factory module to Site Foundry throughout. SEO module rebuilt (Rank Math-style): AI generation now reads each page's REAL content (fixes the 'Page Not Found' metadata bug where it read empty CMS blocks on scanned pages) and writes context-aware, business/location-specific titles + descriptions. Per-page SEO now includes focus keyword with a live on-page analysis checklist + score, canonical URL, robots index/noindex + follow/nofollow, Open Graph + Twitter card fields, a Google snippet preview, and a per-page schema type. New 'Apply to all pages' writes managed meta tags + JSON-LD (site-wide LocalBusiness/Organization + per-page schema) into every page file and regenerates sitemap.xml + robots.txt. Sitemap generator and robots.txt editor added. (Redirections + a 404 monitor are the next SEO addition.) Also fixed the editor toolbar: the Visual/Code toggle is centered and the device buttons no longer break when returning from Code view. Code editor for the page editor: a Visual ⇄ Code toggle in the editor toolbar opens a VS Code-style source view (CodeMirror, syntax-highlighted HTML + inline CSS/JS, dark theme, line numbers, auto-close tags, Ctrl/Cmd+S to save). Switching back to Visual ingests any hand-edits so they stay editable visually; saving in Code mode writes the source directly. Fixed a media-gallery bug where, on a filtered view (e.g. Videos), clicking a tile opened/copied the item at that slot in the unfiltered 'All' list instead of the one you clicked. Editor preview now fills the canvas: the desktop preview is the largest 16:9 box that fits (rendering at a real desktop width — at least 1440, larger on big monitors) plus a Fill-screen button (native fullscreen). Fixed self profile editing — the signed-in user's id and email are now stored in the session so the Account modal populates and saves (was 'Please sign in again'). Added custom code injection points in Analytics: Functional/Analytics/Marketing each with a header and a body field, injected into <head> and before </body> via the existing Apply-to-All-Pages sweep. Account/Settings cleanup: removed the Updates card and the Mailgun card from Settings (Mailgun is now configured server-side in config.secret.php so the API key never passes through the browser); removed the top-right notification bell; the top-right avatar is now a profile menu (Account / Change password / Sign out) and the change-password + sign-out buttons moved out of the bottom-left into it; signed-in users can now edit their own profile (name + email) via the new Account modal. ADAptify panel isolation fix: the widget is now appended to <html> (a sibling of <body>) and every visitor effect is scoped to <body>, so the panel itself can never be recolored, filtered, zoomed, or resized by the page — verified the menu stays readable under High Contrast/Invert. ADAptify full option set: the widget now offers Readable Font, Highlight Titles/Links, Text Magnifier, Content Scaling + Font Size + Line Height + Letter Spacing steppers (±10% per click), text alignment, Dark/Light/High contrast, Monochrome, Invert, High/Low saturation, custom text/title/background colors, Mute Sounds, Hide Images, Reading Guide, Reading Mask, Stop Animations, Highlight on Hover/Click, and Large Cursor — grouped into Content/Color/Navigation sections, all persisted per visitor. Updates are now ALWAYS automatic: the manual update card is removed and every site installs the latest engine at login, so no one can run a stale build (re-introduce a gate before a major release if needed). Security: enforce HTTPS for credentials. A new root .htaccess redirects all HTTP traffic to HTTPS site-wide (proxy-aware to avoid redirect loops), and api.php now refuses sign-in / password / secret actions over plain HTTP as a server-side backstop (localhost exempt; toggle with 'require_https' in config.secret.php). Passwords were already hashed at rest with Argon2id/bcrypt; this closes the in-transit gap. Hotfix (1.4.1): neutralized a literal closing-script sequence in an ADAptify comment that truncated admin/index.html and broke login. ADAptify — a site-wide accessibility widget (contrast, text size, grayscale, invert, readable font, highlight links, big cursor, pause motion) with its own CMS tab. Enable/disable, placement, and brand colors are stored server-side in data/adaptify.json and apply to every page instantly. One-click install drops the widget script across all pages. Plus the 1.3.0 work: server-level page list, automatic server connection, server-side settings, Revisions ledger, faithful HTML import, WAF-proof multipart save, square media picker + 16:9 desktop editor, FAQ/accordion editing, and Privacy Policy generator.
apiNote: 1.14.42 CHANGES api.php + db.php (GoHighLevel lead push): api.php adds cmsGhlConfig/cmsGhlMapContact/cmsGhlPushLead/cmsGhlTest + a 'ghl_test' session action (Super-Admin gated), and send_form now upserts the submission into GHL (POST /contacts/upsert on services.leadconnectorhq.com, Version 2021-07-28) when enabled — best-effort, never blocking the form. db.php adds 'ghl_token' (level 3 / Super-Admin) to fourgeSecretPolicy so the Private Integration Token stores encrypted server-side and never returns to the browser. Config lives in data/site.json { ghl:{ enabled, locationId } } + the encrypted ghl_token. Both files auto-update on sites with config.secret.php (1.14.33+), so this reaches them automatically; hand-deploy only a site still without config.secret.php. Nothing happens until a Super-Admin enables it and pastes a token, so it's inert on every site by default. 1.14.41 CHANGES api.php: reCAPTCHA verification now enforces the v3 score threshold. cmsVerifyRecaptcha reads the 0.0–1.0 'score' from Google's siteverify response and rejects a submission when it falls below the threshold in data/site.json (recaptcha.threshold, default 0.5, set via Plugins → reCAPTCHA); v2 responses carry no score, so they pass on success exactly as before (fully backward-compatible). New helper cmsRecaptchaThreshold() reads that value. api.php auto-updates on sites with config.secret.php (1.14.33+), so this reaches them automatically — hand-deploy only a site still without config.secret.php. No api.php change in 1.14.40 (View now renders the snapshot in an in-CMS modal iframe and Restore uses an in-app confirm instead of browser pop-ups/dialogs — all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates; reads/writes still use the existing read_file/write_file actions, unchanged). No api.php change in 1.14.39 (the top-bar site link, the form Custom-CSS source-of-truth fix, mirroring page revisions into GitHub, and the reCAPTCHA Plugins add-on are ALL in admin/index.html, which auto-updates. The revision GitHub mirror uses direct GitHub API calls from the browser plus the existing read_file/write_file/delete_file actions; the reCAPTCHA server-side verification already lives in api.php and is unchanged). No api.php change in 1.14.38 (editor Forms/Revisions defaults, the Revisions nav gating, and the View pop-up fix are all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates). 1.14.37 CHANGES api.php: when config.secret.php has no mail settings, api.php reads SMTP credentials from a legacy send.php in the web root (parsed as TEXT via fourgeLegacySendConfig — never included/executed), so already-launched sites keep sending form email with no config rewrite; config.secret.php values still win. Auto-updates on sites with config.secret.php (1.14.33+). 1.14.36 CHANGES api.php: the SMTP form sender now resolves 'From' from mg_from (falling back to the SMTP login, never the mg.example.com placeholder that a relay would reject). api.php auto-updates on sites with config.secret.php (1.14.33+), so this reaches them automatically; hand-deploy only a site still without config.secret.php. Also refreshed admin/config.secret.example.php (template only — not deployed). No api.php change in 1.14.35 (Save-now-applies for Analytics + Favicon is all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates; it re-writes pages via the existing read_file/write_file actions). No api.php change in 1.14.34 (form header, visual style controls, and layout blocks are all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates). admin/api.php reads all secrets from config.secret.php (it is secret-free in the repo) and, as of 1.14.33, AUTO-UPDATES like the rest of the engine — BUT only on sites that have config.secret.php (the server refuses to overwrite api.php when that file is missing, so inline-secret installs are never wiped). BOOTSTRAP: deploy 1.14.33's admin/api.php by hand ONCE per site (it carries the safeguard + the send_form public-endpoint fix); after that, future api.php changes install themselves. 1.14.33 CHANGES api.php: adds 'admin/api.php' to fourgeUpdateFetchAllow() (private-repo fetch) and adds the cmsWriteFile safeguard (refuse to overwrite api.php if config.secret.php is absent). No api.php change in 1.14.32 (the side-by-side fix — refreshing the shared form CSS on Save and enforcing the layout rules — is entirely in admin/index.html, which auto-updates; it re-writes pages via the existing write_file action). No api.php change in 1.14.31 (the per-page CSS editor reads a page's existing CSS via the existing read_file action; all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates). No api.php change in 1.14.30 (form-edit propagation + the form CSS editor are in admin/index.html and use the existing read_file/write_file actions). No api.php change in 1.14.29 (form-builder drag/drop, half-width layout, and rounded inputs are all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates). 1.14.28 CHANGES api.php (CRITICAL for forms): 'send_form' is now a PUBLIC action. Public contact-form submissions were returning 401 (visitors have no admin token/session), so no entry was stored and no email sent — forms silently failed on every site. Adding send_form to PUBLIC_ACTIONS lets submissions through (spam is held off by the optional reCAPTCHA inside cmsSendForm). DEPLOY the updated admin/api.php to EVERY site for contact forms to work. No api.php change in 1.14.27 (the orphan-entries fix and the 'convert a page form' feature are entirely in admin/index.html, which auto-updates; conversion writes the page via the existing write_file action). 1.14.26 CHANGES api.php: adds SMTP sending for contact forms. Set smtp_host / smtp_port / smtp_username / smtp_password (and optionally smtp_secure: auto|tls|ssl|none) in config.secret.php; when present, the form handler sends over SMTP instead of the Mailgun HTTP API (From = mg_from, recipient = the form's notify address or mg_notify_to). Credentials stay in config.secret.php (gitignored) — never in the repo. DEPLOY the updated admin/api.php by hand for SMTP to take effect. No api.php change in 1.14.25 (Media Library alt-text popup + auto-apply on save are all in admin/index.html, which auto-updates). No api.php change in 1.14.24 (image alt text is stored in data/media.json and written onto pages client-side by admin/index.html, which auto-updates). No api.php change in 1.14.23 (form submissions were already stored in data/entries.json by the send_form handler; the entries table + CSV export are client-side in admin/index.html, which auto-updates). No api.php change in 1.14.22 (accessibility-widget positioning fix lives in adaptify.js, which the updater auto-pulls). 1.14.21 CHANGES api.php: adds OPTIONAL team onboarding. Set 'onboard_password' (and optionally 'onboard_domain', which defaults to 44interactive.com) in config.secret.php; then a NEW email on that domain that signs in with that password self-provisions an EDITOR account with a forced first-login password change. It never overrides an existing account, never grants above 'editor', and is OFF unless onboard_password is set. Keep that password ONLY in config.secret.php — api.php is public, so a value there would be world-readable. DEPLOY the updated admin/api.php AND add the key(s) to each site's config.secret.php for it to take effect. 1.14.20 CHANGES api.php: it adds the 'install_clean_urls' action, which writes the clean-URL rewrite rule into the site's root .htaccess as a managed block (# BEGIN/END Fourge Clean URLs — coexists with the HTTPS-redirect and password-gate blocks) so extensionless page URLs (/about instead of /about.html) resolve without a hand-edited .htaccess. DEPLOY THE UPDATED admin/api.php by hand to each site; the CMS then calls this automatically on load (once per session) and on every SEO → Apply to all pages, so the extensionless links it writes always have a matching server rule. Until api.php is deployed, add the clean-URL block to the root .htaccess manually. No api.php change in 1.14.19 (the auto-update source repo was re-pointed to the YourDigitalGroup account in admin/index.html only). 1.14.13 adds the Clean URLs (extensionless) feature, always on: the rewrite rules live in the root .htaccess (a managed block that coexists with the HTTPS redirect and the password-gate block). .htaccess is NOT pushed by the auto-updater, so deploy the updated root .htaccess by hand to each site for /about to resolve to /about.html and for /about.html to 301 to /about. Run SEO → Apply to all pages after deploying to rewrite each page's canonicals + internal links to the clean form. (Clean URLs need a host that honors .htaccess mod_rewrite; on a host that can't, the extensionless URLs won't resolve.) No api.php change in 1.14.13. 1.14.7 adds 'data' to the page scanner's SKIP_DIRS so it no longer lists the CMS's own data/ folder (pages.json, site.json, and per-save revision snapshots data/rev_rev_*.html) as importable pages — deploy the updated api.php so revision files never reach the scan; until then the admin filters them client-side. 1.13.0 adds the 'set_page_password' action (per-page password protection): deploy the updated api.php by hand for page protection to work — the gate file (_fourge_gate.php) and .htaccess rules are written automatically when you first protect a page; the bcrypt hashes live in admin/protect.secret.php (never served as source). 1.4.2 adds HTTPS enforcement to api.php (the credential-action backstop) — deploy the updated api.php and the new root .htaccess by hand to get the full effect; the redirect and guard only protect a site once those two files are in place. 1.4.0 adds 'adaptify.js' to api.php's repo-fetch allow-list — only needed for installing the widget from a PRIVATE repo.
